name: Agent
description:
Library component. Models the substrate view of an LLM session (Claude Code, Pi, Codex) running in a tmux pane, captured across context-cycle boundaries.

v0.2 (defense-doc P2.4): Agent now loops Resumed -> Active so multiple substrate cycles can be observed in one TLC run. Three states: Active (predecessor doing work), Compacting (PreCompact fired, runtime is invoking the cycle command), Resumed (successor read the handoff mail via gt prime --hook). After Resumed, the agent transitions back to Active to await the next PreCompact.

The Active->Compacting->Resumed shape compresses the kill+respawn into a single observable transition: from the model's perspective, the agent is the same actor that goes through compaction. Real implementation distinguishes predecessor PID from successor PID, but for the substrate protocol the only thing that matters is whether mail safely persisted before the kill -- which is what the composition's invariants verify.

Real implementation: hooks/config.go DefaultOverrides registers crew PreCompact -> 'gt handoff --cycle --reason compaction'. The hook fires the cycle subprocess (handoff.go:455-591) which respawns the pane (handoff.go:591). The new pane runs the SessionStart hook which is `gt prime --hook`; checkHandoffMarker (prime.go:138) detects the marker and runPrimeCompactResume injects the mail context (prime.go:167-170).

HOOK_FIRES.description: Lifecycle hook fires on this agent. Tagged 'PreCompact' for context-cycle. Real: hooks/config.go DefaultOverrides registers crew PreCompact -> 'gt handoff --cycle --reason compaction'.

TRIGGER_CYCLE.description: After the runtime fires PreCompact, the runtime executes the registered hook command (the cycle subprocess). Modeled here as a direct trigger to HandoffCycle. Real: tmux exec spawns the hook command which is the gt handoff --cycle invocation.

PRIME_FROM_MAIL.description: Successor's SessionStart hook delivers boot signal: gt prime --hook reads the handoff mail and resumes work. Real: prime.go:167-170 runPrimeCompactResume.

actors.name: Agent
actors.owner: Substrate-layer LLM session, looped across cycles. Active is the working state; Compacting is the moment the runtime fires the cycle command; Resumed is post-respawn. After Resumed the agent loops to Active to receive the next PreCompact (multi-cycle support, defense-doc P2.4).

triples.event: PreCompact hook fires -- agent's context window is about to compact
runtime executes the registered cycle command (gt handoff --cycle)
successor's SessionStart hook reads handoff mail; agent resumes work
agent works for a while, ready for next cycle (unconditional loop back to Active)
